```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        legend.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=12),
        legend.position='right')


filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnLAESQNoEnv_glmnetQb"
 
if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=10, height=5,
         path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"), system_fonts = list('serif'="Times New Roman"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=10, height=5, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

##### fit elastic net-informed model

###### formula

```{r}

m3cformula <- update(m3formula, . ~ . - UASImpulsLoudWZAvgMaxLR -
                       UASFluctECMA10ExBin - UASRoughFZ05ExMaxLR)

```

###### run model

####### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m3cformula, data=m3Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```


```{r}

m3c <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m3cformula, data=m3Data,
                          family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                          id=ID,
                          corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m3c, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)


```

###### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m3c.resP <- residuals(m3c, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m3c, residual_vals=m3c.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m3c.resM <- residuals(m3c, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m3c,
                    clusterVar=m3c.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m3c.Cook <- cooks.distance(m3c, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m3c, clusterVar=m3c.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m3c)

```

###### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m3, m3c)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m3Data))

m3c_cv <- cv_glmgee(m3c, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                    data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                    return_predictions=TRUE,
                    backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                          direction="reverse",
                                                          squeeze="none"),
                    workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv))

```

###### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m3Data))

m3cpredMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m3c, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "UASLAEMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m3c, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m3cpredMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m3cpredMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m3cpredMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnLAESQnetCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### export the model

```{r}

# save the model
if (savedata) {
  filename = "dA_LAESQreg_noEnv_Qb"
  saveRDS(m3, file=file.path(outDataPath, paste(filename, ".rds", sep="")))
  
}

```

## with ambient env classification

### LAE, events, env and trial sequence only

#### model 4 formula

```{r}

m4formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl + UASLAEMaxLRScl*I(log10(UASEvents)) +
                                        UASLAEMaxLRScl*AmbientEnv

m4Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', all.vars(m4formula)), drop=FALSE]

```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m4formula, data=m4Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m4 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m4formula, data=m4Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m4, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m4.resP <- residuals(m4, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m4, residual_vals=m4.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m4.resM <- residuals(m4, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m4,
                    clusterVar=m4.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m4.Cook <- cooks.distance(m4, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m4, clusterVar=m4.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m4)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m4Data))

m4_cv <- cv_glmgee(m4, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                                data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                                return_predictions=TRUE,
                                backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                      direction="reverse",
                                                                      squeeze="none"),
                                workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m4Data))

m4predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m4, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "UASLAEMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m4, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m4predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m4predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m4predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnLAEEnvCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

### full SQ model

#### model 5 formula

```{r}

m5formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl + UASLAEMaxLRScl*I(log10(UASEvents)) +
                        UASLAEMaxLRScl*AmbientEnv + UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl*UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl+
                        UASImpulsLoudWZAvgMaxLR + UASFluctECMA10ExBin +
                        UASRoughFZ05ExMaxLR

m5Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', (m5formula)), drop=FALSE]

```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m5formula, data=m5Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m5 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m5formula, data=m5Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m5, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m5.resP <- residuals(m5, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m5, residual_vals=m5.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m5.resM <- residuals(m5, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m5,
                    clusterVar=m5.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m5.Cook <- cooks.distance(m5, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m5, clusterVar=m5.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m5)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m5Data))

m5_cv <- cv_glmgee(m5, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                                data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                                return_predictions=TRUE,
                                backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                      direction="reverse",
                                                                      squeeze="none"),
                                workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m5Data))

m5predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m5, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "UASLAEMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m5, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m5predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m5predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m5predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnLAESQEnvCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### test model interactions

##### stepwise back removal of variables

```{r}

modelStep <- glmtoolbox::stepCriterion(model=m5,
                                       criterion='agpc', test='score',
                                       direction='backward',
                                       varest='bias-corrected')

m5formula
modelStep$final

```

#### regularised version of model with glmnet

```{r}

mf <- model.frame(m5formula, data = m5Data)      # na.omit happens here
kept_rows <- as.integer(rownames(mf))               # original row indices actually retained

y_matrix <- model.response(mf)
X_matrix <- model.matrix(m5formula, data=m5Data)[,-1]

StimID_kept <- m5Data$StimID[kept_rows]

stim_folds <- setNames(
  sample(rep(1:10, length.out = length(unique(StimID_kept)))),
  unique(StimID_kept)
)
foldid <- stim_folds[as.character(StimID_kept)]

stopifnot(length(foldid) == nrow(X_matrix), length(y_matrix) == nrow(X_matrix))


set.seed(808)
m5b <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(x=X_matrix, y=y_matrix, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         alpha=0.5, foldid=foldid, type.measure='deviance', standardize=TRUE,
                         lambda.min.ratio = 1e-18, nlambda = 500)

coef(m5b, s = "lambda.min")
coef(m5b, s = "lambda.1se")

```

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}

# 1. Turn the glmnet path data into a clean data frame
coefficient_paths <- broom::tidy(m5b$glmnet.fit, return_zeros=TRUE) |>
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") # Exclude intercept to keep scale clean

# 3. Create the ggplot
ggplot(coefficient_paths, aes(x = lambda, y = estimate, color = term)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, alpha=0.6) +
  # Use your custom colour palette mycolours as colour scale
  scale_color_manual(values = mycolours) +
  scale_x_log10() + # Lambda spans orders of magnitude, log-scale is best
  # scale_y_log10() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m5b$lambda.min, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m5b$lambda.min*1.25, y = -max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.25, 
           label = expression(paste("Minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m5b$lambda.1se, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m5b$lambda.1se*1.25, y = -max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.25, 
           label = expression(paste("1SE from minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  labs(x = expression(log[10]*(lambda)),
      y = "Coefficient estimate",
      color = "Predictors"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = alpha("grey90", alpha=0.5), linetype='dashed'),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        aspect.ratio=1,
        text = element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        legend.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=12),
        legend.position='right')


filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnLAESQEnv_glmnetQb"
 
if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=10, height=5,
         path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"), system_fonts = list('serif'="Times New Roman"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=10, height=5, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### analyse population-average predictions

```{r}


m5DataFit <- insight::get_data(m5)
m5predSxT <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m5, newdata=m5DataFit,
                                              variables =
                                                list(UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl=seq(
                                                  min(stimDataNoAmb$UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl),
                                                  max(stimDataNoAmb$UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl),
                                                  length.out=5),
                                                  UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl=c(0.3, 0.65, 1, 1.35, 1.7) -
                                                    mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLR),
                                                  UASLAEMaxLRScl=c(-11, -5, 1, 7)),
                                                  by=c("UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl",
                                                       "AmbientEnv", "UASLAEMaxLRScl"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m5, type="bias-corrected"),
                                                  transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                               direction='reverse',
                                                                               squeeze='none'))

m5predSxT <- m5predSxT |> dplyr::rename(UASLAEMaxLRSclRnd=UASLAEMaxLRScl) |>
  dplyr::mutate(UASLAEMaxLRSclRnd=factor(UASLAEMaxLRSclRnd),
                UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl=factor(UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl))

```

#### visualise predictions

```{r}

stimDataNoAmbTemp <- stimDataNoAmb
numbersIn <- stimDataNoAmbTemp$UASLAEMaxLRScl
numberList <- c(-11, -5, 1, 6, 7, 12)
stimDataNoAmbTemp$UASLAEMaxLRSclRnd <- factor(numberSnap(numbersIn, numberList, rangeVal=0.9))
facetLabels <- c('-11'="56", '-5'="62", '1'="68", '6'="73", '7'="74", '12'="79")

ggplot(data=subset(m5predSxT, AmbientEnv=="Park")) +
        theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1.35, text = element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
              axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +

        geom_ribbon(aes(x=UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl, ymin=conf.low,
                        ymax=conf.high, fill=UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl), alpha=0.08) +
        
        geom_line(aes(x=UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl, y=estimate,
                      colour=UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl), alpha=0.45, linewidth=1) +

        scale_color_viridis(option='mako', direction=1, discrete=TRUE, begin = 0.15, end = 0.85,
                            labels=function(x) format(round(as.numeric(x) +
                                                              mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLR), 2), nsmall = 2),
                            guide=guide_legend(title=expression(paste(italic(T)[a*Sigma*5]~","~tu[SHM])),
                                   reverse=TRUE)) +
        scale_fill_viridis(option='mako', direction=1, discrete=TRUE, begin = 0.15, end = 0.85,
                           labels=function(x) format(round(as.numeric(x) +
                                                             mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLR), 2), nsmall = 2),
                           guide=guide_legend(title=expression(paste(italic(T)[a*Sigma*5]~","~tu[SHM])),
                                   reverse=TRUE)) +

        ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
        
        geom_point(data=subset(stimDataNoAmbTemp, SessionPart == "A" & AmbientEnv=="Park"),
                   aes(x=UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl, y=dAnnoyMean,
                       colour=UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl),
                   alpha=0.45, size=2) +

        scale_color_viridis(option='mako', direction=1, discrete=FALSE, begin = 0.15, end = 0.85,
                            limits=c(0.3, 1.7) - mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLR),
                            labels=function(x) format(round(x +
                                                            mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLR), 2), nsmall = 2),
                            guide='none') +

        scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1.3, 2.9, by=0.3) - mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLR),
                           labels=function(x) format(round(x +
                                                           mean(subjDataNoAmb.med$UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLR), 1), nsmall = 1),
                           sec.axis=sec_axis(~ . ,
                                     name=expression(paste(UAS~italic(L)[AE]~","~dB)),
                                     breaks = NULL, labels = NULL)) +
        scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-3, 10, by=1)) +
        
        labs(x=expression(paste(UAS~italic(S)["5"],",",~acum[A~"|"~`M-G-S`])), y="Mean change in annoyance") +
        facet_grid(~ UASLAEMaxLRSclRnd,
                   labeller=labeller(UASLAEMaxLRSclRnd=facetLabels))

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnLAESQEnv_SxT_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=11, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=11, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}


```

## UAS-env loudness diff

### LAEdiff with events

#### model 6 formula

```{r}

m6formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl +
                            I(exp(0.03*LAELAF50diffScl)) +
                            I(log10(UASEvents))

m6Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', all.vars(m6formula)), drop=FALSE]

```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m6formula, data=m6Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m6 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m6formula, data=m6Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m6, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m6.resP <- residuals(m6, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m6, residual_vals=m6.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m6.resM <- residuals(m6, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m6,
                    clusterVar=m6.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m6.Cook <- cooks.distance(m6, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m6, clusterVar=m6.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m6)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5, m6)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m6Data))

m6_cv <- cv_glmgee(m6, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                  data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                  return_predictions=TRUE,
                  backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                        direction="reverse",
                                                        squeeze="none"),
                  workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv,
                        m6=m6_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m6Data))

m6predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m6, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "LAELAF50diffScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m6, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m6predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m6predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m6predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMndLAECal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

### full SQ model

#### model 7 formula

```{r}

m7formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl +
              I(exp(0.03*LAELAF50diffScl)) +
              I(log10(UASEvents)) +
              UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl*UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl +
                                    UASImpulsLoudWZAvgMaxLR + UASFluctECMA10ExBin +
                                    UASRoughFZ05ExMaxLR

m7Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', all.vars(m7formula)), drop=FALSE]
   
```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m7formula, data=m7Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m7 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m7formula, data=m7Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m7, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m7.resP <- residuals(m7, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m7, residual_vals=m7.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m7.resM <- residuals(m7, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m7,
                    clusterVar=m7.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m7.Cook <- cooks.distance(m7, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m7, clusterVar=m7.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m7)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5, m6, m7)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m7Data))

m7_cv <- cv_glmgee(m7, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                  data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                  return_predictions=TRUE,
                  backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                        direction="reverse",
                                                        squeeze="none"),
                  workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv,
                        m6=m6_cv, m7=m7_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m7Data))

m7predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m7, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "LAELAF50diffScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m7, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m7predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m7predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m7predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMndLAESQCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### test model interactions

##### stepwise back removal of variables

```{r}

modelStep <- glmtoolbox::stepCriterion(model=m7,
                                       criterion='agpc', test='score',
                                       direction='backward',
                                       varest='bias-corrected')

m7formula
modelStep$final

```


#### regularised version of model with glmnet

```{r}

mf <- model.frame(m7formula, data = m7Data)      # na.omit happens here
kept_rows <- as.integer(rownames(mf))               # original row indices actually retained

y_matrix <- model.response(mf)
X_matrix <- model.matrix(m7formula, data=m7Data)[,-1]

StimID_kept <- m7Data$StimID[kept_rows]

stim_folds <- setNames(
  sample(rep(1:10, length.out = length(unique(StimID_kept)))),
  unique(StimID_kept)
)
foldid <- stim_folds[as.character(StimID_kept)]

stopifnot(length(foldid) == nrow(X_matrix), length(y_matrix) == nrow(X_matrix))


set.seed(808)
m7b <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(x=X_matrix, y=y_matrix, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         alpha=0.5, foldid=foldid, type.measure='deviance',
                         standardize=TRUE, lambda.min.ratio = 1e-24, nlambda = 500)

coef(m7b, s = "lambda.min")
coef(m7b, s = "lambda.1se")

```

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}

# 1. Turn the glmnet path data into a clean data frame
coefficient_paths <- broom::tidy(m7b$glmnet.fit, return_zeros=TRUE) |>
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") # Exclude intercept to keep scale clean


# 3. Create the ggplot
ggplot(coefficient_paths, aes(x = lambda, y = estimate, color = term)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, alpha=0.6) +
  # Use your custom colour palette mycolours as colour scale
  scale_color_manual(values = mycolours) +
  scale_x_log10() + # Lambda spans orders of magnitude, log-scale is best
  # scale_y_log10() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m7b$lambda.min, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m7b$lambda.min*1.25, y = -max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.25, 
           label = expression(paste("Minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m7b$lambda.1se, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m7b$lambda.1se*1.25, y = -max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.25, 
           label = expression(paste("1SE from minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  labs(x = expression(log[10]*(lambda)),
      y = "Coefficient estimate",
      color = "Predictors"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = alpha("grey90", alpha=0.5), linetype='dashed'),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        aspect.ratio=1,
        text = element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        legend.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=12),
        legend.position='right')

  
filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMdLAESQ_glmnetQb"
 
if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=10, height=5,
         path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"), system_fonts = list('serif'="Times New Roman"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=10, height=5, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### analyse population-average predictions

```{r}

m7DataFit <- insight::get_data(m7)
m7predEvt <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m7, m7DataFit,
                                                    variables = list(LAELAF50diffScl=seq(
                                                      min(stimDataNoAmb$LAELAF50diffScl),
                                                      max(stimDataNoAmb$LAELAF50diffScl),
                                                      length.out=25)),
                                                  by=c("LAELAF50diffScl", "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m1d, type="bias-corrected"),
                                                  transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                               direction='reverse',
                                                                               squeeze='none'))

```

#### visualise predictions

```{r}

ggplot(data=m7predEvt) +
              theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1.35,
                    text = element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
                    axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
              
              geom_point(data=stimDataNoAmb,
                         aes(x=LAELAF50diffScl, y=dAnnoyMean,
                             colour=factor(UASEvents), shape=factor(UASEvents)),
                         alpha=0.55, size=2) +
              
              geom_ribbon(aes(x=LAELAF50diffScl, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high, fill=factor(UASEvents)),
                          alpha=0.15) +
              
              geom_line(aes(x=LAELAF50diffScl, y=estimate, colour=factor(UASEvents)), linewidth=1) +
              
              scale_color_viridis(option='viridis', direction=1, discrete=TRUE, begin = 0.2, end = 0.8,
                                  guide=guide_legend(title="UAS events", reverse=TRUE)) +
              scale_fill_viridis(option='viridis', direction=1, discrete=TRUE, begin = 0.2, end = 0.8,
                                 guide=guide_legend(title="UAS events", reverse=TRUE)) +
              scale_shape_manual(values=c(4, 5, 7, 8), guide=guide_legend(title="UAS events", reverse=TRUE)) +

              rescaleTicks(stimDataNoAmb$LAELAF50diff,
                             stimDataNoAmb$LAELAF50diffScl, sep=5) +
              
              scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-10, 10, by=1), limits=c(-2, 8)) +
              
              labs(x=expression(paste(UAS~italic(L)[AE]~-~amb.~italic(L)[AF50],",",~dB)), y="Mean change in annoyance")

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMndLAESQModEvt_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=5, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=5, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

## UAS detectability

### fit and examine detectability only model

#### model 8 formula

```{r}

m8formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl +
  I(exp(0.035*Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl)) +
  I(log10(UASEvents))

m8Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', all.vars(m8formula)), drop=FALSE]

```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m8formula, data=m8Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m8 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m8formula, data=m8Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m8, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m8.resP <- residuals(m8, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m8, residual_vals=m8.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m8.resM <- residuals(m8, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m8,
                    clusterVar=m8.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m8.Cook <- cooks.distance(m8, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m8, clusterVar=m8.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m8)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5, m6, m7, m8)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m8Data))

m8_cv <- cv_glmgee(m8, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                  data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                  return_predictions=TRUE,
                  backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                        direction="reverse",
                                                        squeeze="none"),
                  workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv,
                        m6=m6_cv, m7=m7_cv, m8=m8_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m8Data))

m8predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m8, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m8, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m8predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m8predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m8predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnDetCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

### full SQ model

#### model 9 formula

```{r}

m9formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl +
  I(exp(0.035*Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl)) +
  I(log10(UASEvents)) +
  UASTonalAwSHMInt05ExMaxLRScl*UASSharpAurISO305ExMaxLRScl +
                        UASImpulsLoudWZAvgMaxLR + UASFluctECMA10ExBin +
                        UASRoughFZ05ExMaxLR

m9Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', all.vars(m9formula)), drop=FALSE]

```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m9formula, data=m9Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m9 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m9formula, data=m9Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m9, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m9.resP <- residuals(m9, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m9, residual_vals=m9.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m9.resM <- residuals(m9, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m9,
                    clusterVar=m9.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m9.Cook <- cooks.distance(m9, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m9, clusterVar=m9.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m9)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5, m6, m7,
                    m8, m9)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m9Data))

m9_cv <- cv_glmgee(m9, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                  data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                  return_predictions=TRUE,
                  backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                        direction="reverse",
                                                        squeeze="none"),
                  workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv,
                        m6=m6_cv, m7=m7_cv, m8=m8_cv, m9=m9_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m9Data))

m9predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m9, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m9, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m9predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m9predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m9predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnDetSQCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### test model interactions

##### stepwise back removal of variables

```{r}

modelStep <- glmtoolbox::stepCriterion(model=m9,
                                       criterion='agpc', test='score',
                                       direction='backward',
                                       varest='bias-corrected')

m9formula
modelStep$final

```

#### regularised version of model with glmnet

```{r}

mf <- model.frame(m9formula, data = m9Data)      # na.omit happens here
kept_rows <- as.integer(rownames(mf))               # original row indices actually retained

y_matrix <- model.response(mf)
X_matrix <- model.matrix(m9formula, data=m9Data)[,-1]

StimID_kept <- m9Data$StimID[kept_rows]

stim_folds <- setNames(
  sample(rep(1:10, length.out = length(unique(StimID_kept)))),
  unique(StimID_kept)
)
foldid <- stim_folds[as.character(StimID_kept)]

stopifnot(length(foldid) == nrow(X_matrix), length(y_matrix) == nrow(X_matrix))


set.seed(808)
m9b <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(x=X_matrix, y=y_matrix, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         alpha=0.5, foldid=foldid, type.measure='deviance',
                         standardize=TRUE, lambda.min.ratio = 1e-12, nlambda = 200)

coef(m9b, s = "lambda.min")
coef(m9b, s = "lambda.1se")

```

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}

# 1. Turn the glmnet path data into a clean data frame
coefficient_paths <- broom::tidy(m9b$glmnet.fit, return_zeros=TRUE) |>
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") # Exclude intercept to keep scale clean


# 3. Create the ggplot
ggplot(coefficient_paths, aes(x = lambda, y = estimate, color = term)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, alpha=0.6) +
  # Use your custom colour palette mycolours as colour scale
  scale_color_manual(values = mycolours) +
  scale_x_log10() + # Lambda spans orders of magnitude, log-scale is best
  # scale_y_log10() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m9b$lambda.min, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m9b$lambda.min*1.25, y = -max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.25, 
           label = expression(paste("Minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m9b$lambda.1se, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m9b$lambda.1se*1.25, y = -max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.25, 
           label = expression(paste("1SE from minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  labs(x = expression(log[10]*(lambda)),
      y = "Coefficient estimate",
      color = "Predictors"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = alpha("grey90", alpha=0.5), linetype='dashed'),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        aspect.ratio=1,
        text = element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        legend.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=12),
        legend.position='right')

  
filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMDetSQ_glmnetQb"
 
if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=10, height=5,
         path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"), system_fonts = list('serif'="Times New Roman"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=10, height=5, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### analyse population-average predictions

```{r}

m9DataFit <- insight::get_data(m9)
m9predEvt <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m9, m9DataFit,
                                                    variables = list(Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl=seq(
                                                      min(stimDataNoAmb$Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl),
                                                      max(stimDataNoAmb$Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl),
                                                      length.out=25)),
                                                  by=c("Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl", "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m1d, type="bias-corrected"),
                                                  transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                               direction='reverse',
                                                                               squeeze='none'))

```

#### visualise predictions

```{r}


ggplot(data=m9predEvt) +
              theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1.35,
                    text = element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
                    axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
              
              geom_point(data=stimDataNoAmb,
                         aes(x=Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl, y=dAnnoyMean,
                             colour=factor(UASEvents), shape=factor(UASEvents)),
                         alpha=0.55, size=2) +
              
              geom_ribbon(aes(x=Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high, fill=factor(UASEvents)),
                          alpha=0.15) +
              
              geom_line(aes(x=Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl, y=estimate, colour=factor(UASEvents)), linewidth=1) +
              
              scale_color_viridis(option='viridis', direction=1, discrete=TRUE, begin = 0.2, end = 0.8,
                                  guide=guide_legend(title="UAS events", reverse=TRUE)) +
              scale_fill_viridis(option='viridis', direction=1, discrete=TRUE, begin = 0.2, end = 0.8,
                                 guide=guide_legend(title="UAS events", reverse=TRUE)) +
              scale_shape_manual(values=c(4, 5, 7, 8), guide=guide_legend(title="UAS events", reverse=TRUE)) +

              rescaleTicks(stimDataNoAmb$Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl,
                             stimDataNoAmb$Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl, sep=7) +
              
              scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-10, 10, by=1), limits=c(-2, 8)) +
              
              labs(x=expression(paste(UAS~italic(d*"'")[Sigma*0.5*s],",",~dB~re.~1)), y="Mean change in annoyance")

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnDetSQModEvt_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=5, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=5, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}



```

### dSQ model

#### model 10 formula

```{r}

m10formula <- dAnnoyanceOrdBetaScl ~ TrialNumberScl +
  I(exp(0.035*Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl)) +
   I(log10(UASEvents)) +
  dTonalSHMIntAvgMaxLR*dSharpAurISO3PowAvgMaxLR +
  dImpulsLoudWZAvgMaxLR + dFluctECMA10ExBin +
  dRoughFZ05ExMaxLR

m10Data <- subjDataNoAmb.med[, c(ID='ID', StimID='StimID', all.vars(m10formula)), drop=FALSE]

```

#### run model

##### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m10formula, data=m10Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```

```{r}

m10 <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m10formula, data=m10Data,
                         family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         id=ID,
                         corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m10, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)

```

#### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m10.resP <- residuals(m10, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m10, residual_vals=m10.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m10.resM <- residuals(m10, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m10,
                    clusterVar=m10.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m10.Cook <- cooks.distance(m10, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m10, clusterVar=m10.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m10)

```

#### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5, m6, m7,
                    m8, m9, m10)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m10Data))

m10_cv <- cv_glmgee(m10, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                  data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                  return_predictions=TRUE,
                  backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                        direction="reverse",
                                                        squeeze="none"),
                  workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv,
                        m6=m6_cv, m7=m7_cv, m8=m8_cv, m9=m9_cv, m10=m10_cv))

```

#### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m10Data))
m10predMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m10, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
                                                       "Detect0p5dBIntMaxLRScl",
                                                       "UASEvents"),
                                                  vcov=vcov(m10, type='bias-corrected'),
                                                 type='response',
                                                transform=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                                             direction='reverse',
                                                                             squeeze='none')) |>
  dplyr::arrange(StimID)

R2means <- 1 - sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - m10predMeans$estimate)^2)/sum((stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean - mean(stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean))^2)

eRMSE <- epsilonRMSE(measured_data=stimDataNoAmb, predicted_data=m10predMeans,
                     measured_target='dAnnoyMean', measured_ci_low='dAnnoyMeanCI_Low',
                     measured_ci_high='dAnnoyMeanCI_High', fitted='estimate')

# using ggplot, plot the predicted fit against the responses in predictMeans
ggplot(data=cbind(m10predMeans,
                  response=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMean,
                  responseCI_Low=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_Low,
                  responseCI_High=stimDataNoAmb$dAnnoyMeanCI_High)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=1, text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  
  geom_abline(aes(intercept=0, slope=1), colour='grey', alpha=0.5) +
  
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate), size=1.75, shape=4, stroke=0.75, colour=mycolours[1], alpha=0.5) +

  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, xmin=responseCI_Low, xmax=responseCI_High,
                            y=estimate), width=0, colour=mycolours[1], alpha=0.1) +
  
  geom_errorbar(mapping=aes(x=response, y=estimate, ymin=conf.low, ymax=conf.high), width=0, colour=mycolours[1], alpha=0.1) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-2, 10, by=1), limits=c(-1, 9)) +
  
  labs(x="Mean change in annoyance (test)", y="Predicted mean change in annoyance") +
  
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6.75, label=deparse(bquote(italic(R)^2 == .(sprintf("%.3f", (round(R2means, 3)))))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0, parse=TRUE) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x=0.1, y=6, label=paste0("RMSE = ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$rmse, 3))), family="serif", size=4, hjust = 0) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = 5.25, label = paste0("RMSE[epsilon] == ", sprintf("%.3f", round(eRMSE$epsilon_rmse, 3))), 
  family = "serif", size = 4, hjust = 0, parse = TRUE)

filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMnDetdSQCal_geeQb"

if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=4, height=4, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

#### test model interactions

##### stepwise back removal of variables

```{r}

modelStep <- glmtoolbox::stepCriterion(model=m10,
                                       criterion='agpc', test='score',
                                       direction='backward',
                                       varest='bias-corrected')

m10formula
modelStep$final

```

#### regularised version of model with glmnet

```{r}

mf <- model.frame(m10formula, data = m10Data)      # na.omit happens here
kept_rows <- as.integer(rownames(mf))               # original row indices actually retained

y_matrix <- model.response(mf)
X_matrix <- model.matrix(m10formula, data=m10Data)[,-1]

StimID_kept <- m10Data$StimID[kept_rows]

stim_folds <- setNames(
  sample(rep(1:10, length.out = length(unique(StimID_kept)))),
  unique(StimID_kept)
)
foldid <- stim_folds[as.character(StimID_kept)]

stopifnot(length(foldid) == nrow(X_matrix), length(y_matrix) == nrow(X_matrix))


set.seed(808)
m10b <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(x=X_matrix, y=y_matrix, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                         alpha=0.5, foldid=foldid, type.measure='deviance',
                         standardize=TRUE, lambda.min.ratio = 1e-100, nlambda = 500)

coef(m10b, s = "lambda.min")
coef(m10b, s = "lambda.1se")

```

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}

# 1. Turn the glmnet path data into a clean data frame
coefficient_paths <- broom::tidy(m10b$glmnet.fit, return_zeros=TRUE) |>
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") # Exclude intercept to keep scale clean


# 3. Create the ggplot
ggplot(coefficient_paths, aes(x = lambda, y = estimate, color = term)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, alpha=0.6) +
  # Use your custom colour palette mycolours as colour scale
  scale_color_manual(values = mycolours) +
  scale_x_log10() + # Lambda spans orders of magnitude, log-scale is best
  # scale_y_log10() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m10b$lambda.min, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m10b$lambda.min*1.25, y = max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.5, 
           label = expression(paste("Minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = m10b$lambda.1se, linetype = 'dotted', color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = m10b$lambda.1se*1.25, y = max(coefficient_paths$estimate) * 0.5, 
           label = expression(paste("1SE from minimum CV-error ",~lambda)), color = "grey", angle = 90, vjust = 0.5) +
  labs(x = expression(log[10]*(lambda)),
      y = "Coefficient estimate",
      color = "Predictors"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = alpha("grey90", alpha=0.5), linetype='dashed'),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        aspect.ratio=1,
        text = element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14),
        legend.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=12),
        legend.position='right')

  
filename <- "PtsABdAnnoyMDetdSQ_glmnetQb"
 
if (saveplots){
  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""), width=10, height=5,
         path=file.path(outFigPath, "svg"), system_fonts = list('serif'="Times New Roman"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".svg", sep=""))

  ggsave(filename=paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""), width=10, height=5, path=file.path(outFigPath, "pdf"))
  unlink(paste(filename, ".pdf", sep=""))
}

```

##### fit elastic net-informed model

###### formula

```{r}

m10cformula <- update(m10formula, . ~ . - dFluctECMA10ExBin)

```

###### run model

####### check correlation structure

```{r}

geeCorStr(m10cformula, data=m10Data, family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
           id=ID, maxit=2e3, verbose=FALSE)

```


```{r}

m10c <- glmtoolbox::glmgee(m10cformula, data=m10Data,
                          family=quasibinomial(link='logit'),
                          id=ID,
                          corstr='exchangeable')

geeParams(m10c, ci=0.95, exponentiate=FALSE, varest='bias-corrected', verbose=FALSE)


```

###### model diagnostics

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}}

m10c.resP <- residuals(m10c, type="pearson", plot.it=FALSE,
                      pch=1, col=mycolours[3], identify=10)

gee_residplot(m10c, residual_vals=m10c.resP,
              colour=mycolours[3], alpha=0.3) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4, text=element_text (family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Fitted values", y="Standardised Pearson residuals")

m10c.resM <- residuals(m10c, type="mahalanobis", plot.it=FALSE)

# plot Mahalanobis residuals with ggplot2 on a stem plot replacing the index by the corresponding participant ID
geeCluster_stemplot(m10c,
                    clusterVar=m10c.resM,
                    colour=mycolours[2], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Mahalanobis distance")

m10c.Cook <- cooks.distance(m10c, plot.it=FALSE)

geeCluster_stemplot(m10c, clusterVar=m10c.Cook,
                    colour=mycolours[4], alpha=0.7) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), aspect.ratio=0.4,
        text=element_text(family = "serif", size=16),
        axis.text=element_text(family = "serif", size=14)) +
  labs(x="Participant ID", y="Cook's distance")

glmgee_vif(m10c)

```

###### analyse performance

```{r}

geeModelPerformance(m1d, m2, m3, m3c, m4, m5, m6, m7,
                    m8, m9, m10, m10c)

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m10Data))
m10c_cv <- cv_glmgee(m10c, id_col="ID", K=10, seed=cv_seed,
                    data=nd, agg_cols="StimID",
                    return_predictions=TRUE,
                    backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                                          direction="reverse",
                                                          squeeze="none"),
                    workers=n_cores) |>

          cv_aggregated_metrics(backtransform_fn=\(x) betaTransform(x, -10, 10,
                                "reverse", squeeze="none"))

compare_cv_results(list(m1d=m1d_cv, m2=m2_cv, m3=m3_cv, m3c=m3c_cv, m4=m4_cv, m5=m5_cv,
                        m6=m6_cv, m7=m7_cv, m8=m8_cv, m9=m9_cv, m10=m10_cv, m10c=m10c_cv))

```

###### analyse and visualise calibration

```{r}

nd <- na.omit(cbind(StimID = subjDataNoAmb.med$StimID, m10Data))

m10cpredMeans <- marginaleffects::avg_predictions(m10c, newdata=nd,
                                                  by=c("StimID",
```
